title: Living in a Precarious World
subtitle: Impermanence, Resilience, Awakening
about: The largest gathering in Sakyadhita's history. More than 3,000 Buddhist monastics, laypeople, and dignitaries from over 30 countries convened in Seoul to explore impermanence, resilience, and awakening in a world reshaped by pandemic, conflict, and climate crisis. Held at the Coex Convention Center in Gangnam, the five-day program featured plenary lectures, workshops, cultural performances, and a temple stay program at historic Korean monasteries.

coverage.headline: Daughters of the Buddha: 18th Sakyadhita Conference in Seoul Celebrates the Sacred Feminine
coverage.excerpt: Buddhist monastics and laywomen from around the world gathered in Seoul for the largest conference in Sakyadhita's history — a shining example of the alignment between Buddhist scholarship, activism, and practice. Photos by Olivier Adam.
